{"key_event":"War and revolutionary-state consolidation","reasoning":"The Iran-Iraq War, IRGC/Basij expansion, wartime economy, clerical institutions, and 1989 constitutional changes pushed the Islamic Republic into the strong-state range, with pervasive coercive and administrative reach. Independent parties, unions, universities, ethnic movements, press, and opposition networks were crushed, leaving civil society much weaker despite large state-directed mass mobilization.","society_change":-3.5,"society_power":3.0,"state_change":2.2,"state_power":8.0}
